notableContracts: Multi‑year U.S. Department of Defense and service‑branch contracts for RF, microwave, and electronic warfare test equipment and sustainment support
Framework and IDIQ agreements with major U.S. defense primes for radar, electronic warfare, and secure communications test solutions across air, land, sea, and space programs
Global contracts with NATO and allied defense ministries to supply test systems for satellite communications, avionics, and secure tactical communications modernization initiatives

name: TTM Technologies
description:
TTM Technologies is a leading global manufacturer of printed circuit boards (PCBs), RF and microwave assemblies, and advanced electronic interconnect solutions serving defense, aerospace, and high-reliability commercial markets. The company provides complex, high-layer-count, rigid, rigid-flex, and HDI PCBs, as well as advanced packaging, substrates, and specialty RF components that are integrated into mission-critical systems such as radars, electronic warfare suites, missiles, avionics, and communications networks. TTM’s products are engineered to withstand extreme environmental and performance requirements, making them well-suited for defense, space, and secure communications applications.

Headquartered in Santa Ana, California, TTM Technologies has grown through a series of strategic acquisitions and organic expansion to become one of the largest PCB manufacturers in North America with a significant global footprint. The company plays a key enabling role in U.S. and allied defense programs by supplying high-reliability electronics for platforms across air, land, sea, and space domains. TTM’s defense portfolio is closely aligned with radar modernization, C4ISR, missile defense, and electronic warfare initiatives, supporting prime contractors and system integrators with domestic, ITAR-compliant manufacturing, advanced engineering support, and long-term lifecycle sustainment of critical electronic hardware.
keyProducts: High-reliability rigid and HDI printed circuit boards for defense and aerospace systems
RF and microwave PCBs and assemblies for radar, EW, and secure communications
Advanced IC substrates and specialty substrates for high-speed and high-power applications
Ruggedized PCB solutions for missiles, guided munitions, and avionics
Space-qualified PCB and interconnect solutions for satellites and space payloads
notableContracts: Long-term supply agreements with major U.S. defense primes for radar and electronic warfare PCB content on airborne and ground-based platforms
Production and sustainment contracts to provide high-reliability PCBs for U.S. and allied missile and guided munitions programs
Multi-year contracts supporting secure communications, C4ISR, and space systems through ITAR-compliant PCB manufacturing for U.S. Department of Defense programs

name: Teledyne Technologies
description:
Teledyne Technologies Incorporated is a diversified industrial and defense technology company specializing in instrumentation, digital imaging, aerospace and defense electronics, and engineered systems. The company provides high-performance sensors, cameras, instrumentation, and communication systems used in surveillance, reconnaissance, electronic warfare, undersea warfare, and space applications. Teledyne’s products support U.S. and allied military customers across domains, including airborne ISR payloads, maritime and subsea systems, and spaceborne imaging and sensing solutions.

Formed in 1999 through the spin-off of Teledyne from Allegheny Teledyne, the company traces its heritage back to the original Teledyne, Inc., founded in 1960 by Henry Singleton. Over decades, Teledyne has grown through targeted acquisitions—particularly in digital imaging and marine instrumentation—to become a major supplier of advanced sensors and electronics for defense, intelligence, and aerospace markets. Today, Teledyne plays a key role in supplying mission-critical components and systems for programs such as unmanned underwater vehicles, tactical data links, space imaging payloads, and high-reliability electronics, while also serving industrial, scientific, and environmental markets worldwide.
keyProducts: Teledyne FLIR military and industrial imaging systems
Teledyne DALSA and Teledyne e2v high-performance image sensors and cameras
Teledyne marine and subsea systems (sonar, acoustic sensors, AUV components)
Aerospace and defense microwave/RF components and communications subsystems
Space-qualified imaging and sensing payload components
notableContracts: U.S. Navy and allied navies contracts for undersea warfare and maritime imaging/sonar systems
U.S. Department of Defense programs for infrared and electro-optical sensors supporting ISR and targeting
Space and Earth observation imaging sensor supply contracts for U.S. government and international space agencies

name: StandardAero
description:
StandardAero is a leading independent provider of maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) services for aircraft engines, airframes, and components, serving commercial, business aviation, and defense customers worldwide. The company supports a broad range of turbine engines and aircraft platforms, providing engine overhaul, component repair, engineering, test services, and fleet management solutions. Its customers include air forces, naval aviation units, government agencies, and major defense prime contractors that rely on StandardAero to sustain mission-ready fleets.

Founded in the early 20th century and expanded through a series of acquisitions and facility growth in North America and Europe, StandardAero has become a key aftermarket support partner for military aircraft and helicopter fleets, particularly for legacy and rotary-wing platforms. In the defense sector, the company provides depot-level maintenance, performance upgrades, and life-extension services on engines such as the T56, CF34, and Rolls-Royce and Pratt & Whitney military derivatives, helping defense customers improve readiness, reduce lifecycle cost, and extend the service life of critical aircraft. Although privately held, StandardAero operates at a scale comparable to major OEM-affiliated MRO providers and plays a significant role in sustaining global military airworthiness.

StandardAero’s defense work spans heavy maintenance, component repair, engineering modifications, and on-site field support for U.S. and allied forces. With multiple FAA and military certifications and strategically located facilities in the U.S., Canada, and Europe, the company supports both fixed-wing and rotary platforms used in transport, patrol, special operations, and training missions. Its combination of OEM-authorized capabilities, engineering depth, and global logistics support makes it an important partner in the defense aerospace sustainment ecosystem.
keyProducts: Military turbine engine MRO services (e.g., T56, CF34, F404/F414 derivatives)
Rotary-wing support for military helicopter fleets (e.g., UH-60, CH-47, maritime helicopters)
Component repair and overhaul for fuel systems, accessories, and avionics line-replaceable units
Engineering and modernization programs for life extension and performance upgrades on legacy aircraft
Fleet management and power-by-the-hour support solutions for defense aviation customers
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force and international C-130/T56 engine maintenance and overhaul support contracts
Long-term support agreements for CF34 and other engines powering military special mission and training aircraft
Depot-level maintenance and component repair programs for U.S. and allied military helicopter operators

name: Kratos Defense and Security Solutions
description:
Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, Inc. is a mid-tier U.S. defense contractor specializing in unmanned systems, space and satellite communications, and training and simulation solutions. The company focuses on affordable, rapidly deployable systems that support national security missions, particularly in contested and high-threat environments. Kratos is best known for its tactical unmanned aerial systems, target drones, and space communications infrastructure, serving the U.S. Department of Defense, intelligence community, and allied nations.

Originally formed through a series of acquisitions and reorganizations in the late 1990s and early 2000s, Kratos has evolved from an IT and security services provider into a technology-focused aerospace and defense firm. Its portfolio spans high-performance jet-powered unmanned aerial vehicles, hypersonic and missile defense targets, satellite ground systems, secure communications, and training and simulation platforms. Kratos plays a growing role in emerging missions such as loyal wingman concepts, attritable UAVs, resilient space architectures, and electronic warfare-enabled range and test solutions, positioning itself as an agile alternative to larger prime contractors.
keyProducts: XQ-58A Valkyrie tactical unmanned aerial vehicle
Air Wolf and other jet-powered tactical UAS family
OpenSpace and related satellite ground system software
Space and satellite communications and RF monitoring systems
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory programs for XQ-58A Valkyrie and attritable UAV development
U.S. Navy and Missile Defense Agency contracts for BQM-series aerial target systems and range support
U.S. Space Force and other government contracts for satellite ground systems, command-and-control, and secure space communications

name: Spirit AeroSystems
description:
Spirit AeroSystems is one of the world’s largest non-OEM designers and manufacturers of aerostructures for commercial and defense aircraft. The company produces major structural components such as fuselages, pylons, nacelles, and wing structures for leading aircraft platforms, leveraging advanced composite materials and high-rate manufacturing capabilities. While the bulk of its revenue comes from commercial aerospace, Spirit maintains a significant and growing defense business serving U.S. and allied military customers.

The company traces its roots to Boeing’s Wichita division, which was acquired by investment firm Onex and relaunched as Spirit AeroSystems in 2005. Headquartered in Wichita, Kansas, Spirit supports a range of defense programs including fighter, bomber, tanker, rotorcraft, and ISR platforms, often acting as a key Tier 1 supplier to major primes such as Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and Northrop Grumman. Its defense role centers on delivering complex, mission-critical aerostructures, sustainment support, and engineering services that enhance aircraft survivability, performance, and readiness across the U.S. Department of Defense and international fleets.
keyProducts: Fuselage and major aerostructures for Boeing P-8A Poseidon maritime patrol aircraft
Aerostructures and components for Boeing KC-46A Pegasus tanker
Composite and metallic structures for various U.S. and allied fighter and bomber platforms (including F-15 and B-52 modernization programs)
Rotorcraft aerostructures and assemblies for military helicopter programs
Space-qualified composite structures and components for launch and space systems
notableContracts: Aerostructures supplier role on the Boeing P-8A Poseidon program for the U.S. Navy and international customers
Production and supply of major structural components for the Boeing KC-46A Pegasus tanker program
Long-term agreements with Boeing Defense and other primes to provide defense and space aerostructures, including support for modernization and sustainment of legacy U.S. Air Force platforms

name: Anduril
description:
Anduril Industries is a U.S.-based defense technology company that develops autonomous systems, software, and sensors for national security, border security, and military customers. Built around a software-first model, Anduril integrates artificial intelligence, computer vision, and sensor fusion into a unified operating system known as Lattice, which coordinates a wide range of unmanned systems and surveillance assets in real time. The company positions itself as a new kind of prime contractor, aiming to deliver defense capabilities on commercial timelines with rapid iteration and fielding.

Founded by technologists and entrepreneurs with roots in the commercial tech sector, Anduril has grown quickly by focusing on counter–intrusion, base defense, maritime domain awareness, and autonomous air systems. It has become a significant player in autonomous ISR (intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance), counter‑UAS, and unmanned surface and subsurface vehicles. Through partnerships with the U.S. Department of Defense, U.S. Department of Homeland Security, and allied nations, Anduril contributes to modernizing defense architectures with networked sensors, autonomous platforms, and AI-enabled command and control tools.
keyProducts: Lattice autonomous sensor fusion and command-and-control platform
Sentry Tower and fixed-site surveillance systems
Ghost family of autonomous small UAS
ALTIUS loitering munition and tube-launched UAS systems
Dive-LD and other autonomous underwater/uncrewed maritime systems
notableContracts: U.S. Department of Defense contracts for counter-intrusion and autonomous surveillance systems at bases and critical sites
U.S. Department of Homeland Security/U.S. Customs and Border Protection contracts for border surveillance and integrated sensor towers
U.S. and allied defense ministry awards for Lattice-based autonomous systems, including software and UAS for mission-level autonomy and ISR

name: M1 Support Services
description:
M1 Support Services, L.P. is a U.S.-based defense contractor specializing in aviation maintenance, logistics, and base operations support for the U.S. Department of Defense. The company focuses heavily on contractor logistics support (CLS), aircraft maintenance, modification, and inspection services for fixed- and rotary-wing fleets, particularly within the U.S. Air Force and Army. M1’s work spans organizational- through depot-level maintenance, flight line support, corrosion control, and component repair, ensuring high mission-capable rates for training and operational aircraft.

Founded as a privately held firm, M1 has grown into a major player in the defense services sector by providing cost-effective, performance-based maintenance and logistics solutions at military installations across the United States and overseas. The company is best known for supporting pilot training fleets, including T-38 and T-6 aircraft, as well as helicopter and special mission platforms. Its capabilities extend to supply chain management, technical data management, and program management, making M1 a key enabler of readiness and sustainment for U.S. and allied air forces.

M1’s role in defense is centered on long-term maintenance and logistics contracts that keep critical training and operational assets available, safe, and compliant with stringent military standards. Through integrated logistics support, scheduled and unscheduled maintenance, and on-site field teams, M1 helps reduce lifecycle costs while maximizing aircraft availability. The company’s 100% defense-oriented revenue profile reflects its deep integration into U.S. military aviation sustainment and base support missions.
keyProducts: Aviation maintenance and contractor logistics support (CLS) for U.S. Air Force training fleets
Organizational, intermediate, and limited depot-level maintenance for fixed- and rotary-wing aircraft
Base operations and flight line support services at U.S. and overseas installations
Aircraft corrosion control, structural repair, and modification services
Supply chain, parts management, and technical data support for aviation programs
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force T-38/T-6 aviation maintenance and logistics support contracts at multiple training bases
Aviation maintenance and support services for rotary-wing and special mission aircraft for U.S. Army and Air Force customers
Base operations, flight line, and equipment maintenance support contracts at key U.S. military installations

name: Mercury Systems
description:
Mercury Systems is a U.S.-based technology company specializing in secure, mission-critical processing solutions for aerospace and defense applications. The company designs and manufactures ruggedized embedded computing, RF and microwave components, edge processing systems, and secure mission systems that are integrated into radar, electronic warfare, C4ISR, and weapons platforms. Its offerings are used across air, land, sea, and space domains, providing high‑performance signal and data processing close to the sensor to enable real-time decision-making.

Founded in 1981, Mercury has grown both organically and through acquisitions to become a key supplier to prime contractors and U.S. government programs, focusing heavily on open systems architectures, trusted manufacturing, and secure supply chains within the United States. The company positions itself as a “commercial technology for defense” provider, rapidly adapting advanced commercial semiconductor and computing technologies for SWaP‑constrained, harsh military environments. Mercury’s systems and components support a variety of critical programs including next‑generation radars, avionics upgrades, electronic warfare suites, and space payload processing, reinforcing its role as an important technology partner in the defense industrial base.
keyProducts: Mission computing and avionics processing systems for airborne platforms
Rugged embedded processing modules (OpenVPX, SOSA-aligned) for radar and EW
RF and microwave components and subsystems for electronic warfare and radar
Secure, trusted-built defense electronics and Trusted Microelectronics services
Edge signal and sensor processing systems for C4ISR and EO/IR applications
notableContracts: Radar and mission-computing subsystems for the Patriot and next-generation ground-based radar programs

RF, digital processing, and secure payload electronics for classified U.S. DoD and intelligence community radar, missile defense, and space-based sensor programs

name: AM General
description:
AM General LLC is an American defense and automotive manufacturer best known as the original designer and producer of the High Mobility Multipurpose Wheeled Vehicle (HMMWV or Humvee). The company specializes in light tactical and military vehicles, providing design, engineering, manufacturing, and sustainment services to the U.S. Department of Defense and allied militaries worldwide. In addition to its legacy Humvee fleet, AM General has evolved into a modern tactical vehicle provider with updated platforms designed for increased survivability, payload, and mobility.

Headquartered in Indiana, AM General traces its roots back through several corporate predecessors in the mid-20th century automotive and defense sectors, with the current AM General entity commonly associated with the late 1960s/early 1970s. Over decades of conflict and peacetime operations, its vehicles have become ubiquitous in U.S. and allied force structures, serving as workhorses for transport, command and control, and specialized mission roles. Today, AM General focuses on recapitalization and modernization of the global Humvee fleet, new-generation Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV) production, and tailored vehicle solutions for international customers, while providing lifecycle support, training, and logistics for its platforms.
keyProducts: HMMWV (High Mobility Multipurpose Wheeled Vehicle / Humvee) family of vehicles
JLTV A2 (Joint Light Tactical Vehicle) production for U.S. and allies
Humvee Saber and other up-armored/missionized variants
Humvee sustainment, recapitalization, and modernization programs

Foreign Military Sales (FMS) contracts supplying Humvee variants and support to multiple allied nations in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia

name: Hexcel Corporation
description:
Hexcel Corporation is a leading advanced composites company that designs and manufactures high‑performance structural materials for aerospace, defense, and industrial applications. Its portfolio includes carbon fiber, woven reinforcements, honeycomb core, prepregs, and engineered composite structures that enable lighter, stronger, and more fuel‑efficient aircraft and defense platforms. Hexcel’s materials are widely used in primary and secondary structures on fixed‑wing aircraft, rotorcraft, engines, missiles, UAVs, and space systems, where weight reduction and high strength‑to‑weight ratios are critical.

Founded in the mid‑20th century to supply lightweight materials for emerging aerospace needs, Hexcel has grown into a key supplier to major OEMs and Tier 1 integrators, including those serving the U.S. Department of Defense and allied militaries. The company’s defense role centers on enabling advanced platforms—such as fighter aircraft, transport aircraft, military helicopters, and missile systems—through composite materials that improve performance, survivability, and lifecycle efficiency. With a global manufacturing footprint and strong R&D investment, Hexcel continues to drive innovations in out‑of‑autoclave processing, resin systems, and integrated composite structures that support next‑generation military air and space programs.
keyProducts: HexPly prepreg systems for military and commercial aircraft structures
HexForce carbon and glass fiber reinforcements for aerospace applications
HexWeb honeycomb core materials for aircraft and rotorcraft
Engineered composite structures for nacelles, control surfaces, and fuselage components
Advanced carbon fiber materials for space and launch vehicle structures
notableContracts: Long-term supply agreements providing composite materials for Lockheed Martin and Boeing military aircraft programs, including fighters and transport platforms
Material supply contracts supporting rotorcraft programs such as the H-60 Black Hawk and other U.S. and allied military helicopters
Composite materials supply for space and launch customers, including structures on satellite platforms and launch vehicle fairings used in U.S. and European government space missions

name: Mercury Systems
description:
Mercury Systems is a U.S.-based technology company that specializes in secure, sensor and processing solutions for the aerospace and defense industry. The company focuses on integrating commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) technologies into rugged, SWaP-optimized (size, weight and power) systems that can be deployed on platforms across land, air, sea, cyber, and space. Its offerings span embedded computing, RF and microwave components, mission processing, edge AI, and secure data storage, all designed to meet stringent military and aerospace requirements.

Founded in the early 1980s, Mercury Systems has grown through both organic development and strategic acquisitions, expanding its portfolio in RF, microelectronics, and secure processing. The company plays a key role in modernizing mission-critical systems for the U.S. Department of Defense and allied nations, enabling advanced radar, electronic warfare, signals intelligence, and avionics applications. Positioned as a trusted partner for defense primes and government customers, Mercury focuses on open systems architectures, trusted supply chains within the U.S., and rapid technology insertion to keep pace with evolving threats and mission needs.
keyProducts: OpenVPX and SOSA-aligned embedded processing systems for radar and EW
RF and microwave components and subsystems for electronic warfare and radar
Secure solid-state data storage and encryption solutions for mission systems
Rugged edge AI and signal processing platforms for ISR applications

RF and digital signal processing modules supporting U.S. electronic warfare and signals intelligence modernization efforts
Secure processing and storage solutions for classified mission systems on U.S. military aircraft and unmanned platforms

name: AM General
description:
AM General LLC is a U.S.-based defense contractor best known as the original designer and manufacturer of the High Mobility Multipurpose Wheeled Vehicle (HMMWV or Humvee). The company specializes in tactical wheeled vehicles for military and specialized commercial applications, providing design, engineering, production, and sustainment services for light and medium-duty platforms. AM General supports U.S. and allied forces worldwide with vehicles tailored for troop transport, command and control, medical evacuation, and weapons platforms.

Founded as part of the Studebaker Corporation’s defense operations and later evolving through various ownership structures, AM General has played a pivotal role in U.S. military mobility since the late 20th century. The Humvee became an iconic symbol of U.S. ground forces from the 1980s onward, and AM General has continued to modernize the platform with survivability, payload, and mobility enhancements. Today, the company focuses on next-generation tactical vehicles, including variants of the Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV), and offers lifecycle support, training, and modernization services to both U.S. Department of Defense customers and international partners.
keyProducts: HMMWV (High Mobility Multipurpose Wheeled Vehicle / Humvee) family

name: Hexcel Corporation
description:
Hexcel Corporation is a leading advanced composites and engineered materials company serving the global aerospace, defense, and industrial markets. The company designs and manufactures carbon fiber, reinforcement fabrics, prepregs, honeycomb, and composite structures that are used extensively in military aircraft, helicopters, engines, missiles, space systems, and naval applications. Its lightweight, high-strength materials enable improved range, payload, survivability, and fuel efficiency for modern defense platforms.

Founded as a materials innovator in the mid-20th century, Hexcel has become a core supplier to major prime contractors and OEMs worldwide, including for next-generation fighter aircraft, transport aircraft, rotorcraft, and space launch vehicles. The company’s products are embedded in key U.S. and allied defense programs, often as mission‑critical structural and engine components. Hexcel continues to invest heavily in research and development to advance composite technology, additive manufacturing, and high‑temperature materials, supporting the evolving needs of aerospace and defense customers while expanding into new space and advanced air mobility markets.

Carbon fiber reinforcements for fighter and transport aircraft primary structures
Engineered honeycomb core for radomes, control surfaces, and interior structures

name: ManTech International
description:
ManTech International Corporation is a U.S.-based technology company that provides mission-focused solutions and services to the defense, intelligence, and federal civilian markets. The company specializes in cybersecurity, data analytics, software development, systems engineering, and IT modernization for national security customers. ManTech supports the full lifecycle of critical programs across the Department of Defense, Intelligence Community, and federal agencies.

Founded in 1968, ManTech has grown into a major mid-tier defense contractor with deep expertise in classified environments and mission-critical operations. The company plays a key role in cyber defense, enterprise IT, and intelligence analysis, helping customers protect networks, exploit data, and maintain operational advantage in contested environments.
keyProducts: Cyber operations and defensive cyber services
Enterprise IT modernization and cloud services
notableContracts: Defense Information Systems Agency enterprise IT support contracts
Intelligence Community cyber and mission support programs
U.S. Army and Navy IT modernization and network defense contracts

name: Maximus
description:
Maximus is a leading provider of government services that operates health and human services programs for federal, state, and local governments. While primarily known for administering citizen services programs, Maximus has a significant federal segment that provides IT modernization, data analytics, and digital transformation services to defense and civilian agencies. The company helps government customers improve program outcomes through technology-enabled solutions.

Founded in 1975, Maximus has evolved from a program management consultancy into a large-scale government services contractor with operations across the United States and internationally. In the federal space, Maximus supports defense health programs, benefits administration, and enterprise IT initiatives, leveraging its expertise in large-scale citizen engagement and digital services delivery.
notableContracts: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services program support
Department of Defense health program administration
Federal civilian agency IT modernization contracts

name: ICF International
description:
ICF International is a global consulting and technology services company that provides advisory, implementation, and managed services to government and commercial clients. The company's federal practice supports defense, homeland security, and civilian agencies with strategic consulting, digital transformation, data analytics, and program management. ICF specializes in complex policy and technology challenges across energy, environment, health, and national security domains.

Founded in 1969, ICF has evolved from an energy and environmental consultancy into a diversified professional services firm with strong capabilities in IT modernization, cybersecurity, and analytics. The company serves defense customers with mission support, enterprise IT, and specialized advisory services that help agencies modernize operations and improve outcomes.
notableContracts: Department of Homeland Security program support contracts
Department of Defense advisory and analytics services
Federal civilian agency IT modernization programs

name: Serco Inc.
description:
Serco Inc. is the Americas division of Serco Group plc, a leading provider of public services to government customers. In the United States, Serco delivers defense, federal civilian, and healthcare services including program management, logistics, training, and technical support. The company supports critical missions across the Department of Defense, Department of Homeland Security, and various civilian agencies.

With roots tracing back to the 1920s through predecessor organizations, Serco has developed deep expertise in managing complex government programs and delivering essential services. The company provides operations and maintenance, logistics support, IT services, and specialized technical services to defense customers, playing a key role in military readiness and installation support.
keyProducts: Defense logistics and supply chain services
notableContracts: U.S. Navy fleet readiness and logistics support
Department of Homeland Security program management
Defense Health Agency healthcare administration

name: Anduril Industries
description:
Anduril Industries is a defense technology company that builds advanced autonomous systems, artificial intelligence platforms, and integrated command-and-control solutions for the U.S. military and allied forces. Founded by Palmer Luckey, Anduril has rapidly emerged as a leading non-traditional defense contractor, developing products including autonomous drones, counter-unmanned aircraft systems, surveillance towers, and the Lattice software platform that fuses sensor data and enables autonomous operations.

The company represents a new model of defense contracting, emphasizing rapid product development, commercial-style software iteration, and venture-backed growth. Anduril's technology stack powers autonomous systems across air, ground, and maritime domains, providing warfighters with persistent surveillance, autonomous targeting, and integrated battlespace awareness. With a valuation exceeding $30 billion, Anduril has become one of the most valuable private defense companies and a key partner in Pentagon modernization efforts.
notableContracts: U.S. Special Operations Command counter-UAS systems
U.S. Marine Corps autonomous ISR programs
U.S. Navy counter-drone and maritime surveillance contracts

name: Shield AI
description:
Shield AI is a defense technology company that develops autonomous aircraft and AI pilot systems for military applications. The company's flagship product, Hivemind, is an AI pilot capable of flying aircraft autonomously in contested environments without GPS, communications, or human intervention. Shield AI focuses on transforming existing aircraft into autonomous platforms and developing purpose-built autonomous systems for intelligence, surveillance, and combat missions.

Founded in 2015 by former Navy SEALs and AI researchers, Shield AI has grown rapidly through venture funding and defense contracts. The company's technology enables autonomous reconnaissance, teaming between manned and unmanned aircraft, and swarm operations. Shield AI represents the next generation of defense technology companies that prioritize AI and autonomy as core capabilities for future warfare.
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force autonomous aircraft programs
U.S. Special Operations Command ISR support
Department of Defense AI and autonomy development contracts

name: Palantir Technologies
description:
Palantir Technologies is a software company that builds data integration and analytics platforms for government and commercial customers. The company's Gotham platform serves defense and intelligence customers, enabling analysts to integrate disparate data sources, conduct complex investigations, and support operational decision-making. Palantir's technology has become embedded in critical defense and intelligence workflows across the U.S. government and allied nations.

Founded in 2003 with backing from the CIA's venture arm In-Q-Tel, Palantir has evolved from a counterterrorism analytics tool into a broad platform for operational AI and data-driven decision-making. The company's defense work spans intelligence analysis, logistics optimization, battlefield awareness, and AI-enabled targeting support. Palantir's Foundry platform extends these capabilities to commercial customers while its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) enables large language model deployment in secure environments.
keyProducts: Gotham data integration and analysis platform
notableContracts: U.S. Army Distributed Common Ground System integration
U.S. Special Operations Command analytics support
Intelligence Community data integration and analysis programs

name: Sierra Nevada Corporation
description:
Sierra Nevada Corporation (SNC) is a privately held aerospace and defense company that provides aircraft modification, tactical systems integration, space systems, and commercial solutions to the U.S. government and international customers. The company is known for its work on intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) platforms, special mission aircraft, and the Dream Chaser spaceplane. SNC serves as a major systems integrator for the Department of Defense, NASA, and allied nations.

Founded in 1963, SNC has grown from a small electronics company into a diversified aerospace and defense contractor with capabilities spanning aircraft modification, C4ISR systems, space vehicles, and propulsion. The company plays a significant role in ISR modernization, providing modified aircraft and integrated sensor systems for military and intelligence customers worldwide.
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force Survivable Airborne Operations Center (SAOC)
U.S. Army High Accuracy Detection and Exploitation System (HADES)
NASA Commercial Resupply Services with Dream Chaser

name: Mercury Systems
description:
Mercury Systems is a leading provider of mission-critical processing subsystems and components for defense and aerospace applications. The company specializes in trusted, secure processing for radar, electronic warfare, C4I, and missile systems, delivering rugged computing solutions that operate in the most demanding environments. Mercury serves as a key supplier to major defense prime contractors and directly to military customers.

Founded in 1981, Mercury has evolved from a signal processing company into a trusted defense electronics supplier with deep expertise in secure processing, sensor systems, and advanced packaging. The company plays a critical role in the defense supply chain, providing the processing power and secure electronics that enable modern weapons systems, radar, and communication platforms.
notableContracts: F-35 Joint Strike Fighter processing subsystems
Patriot missile system electronics upgrades
Multiple radar and EW platform processing contracts

name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
description:
Kratos Defense & Security Solutions is a technology company focused on unmanned systems, satellite communications, microwave electronics, and missile defense. The company develops high-performance tactical drones, target drones for military training, and advanced electronics for radar and communication systems. Kratos is a leader in affordable, attritable unmanned aerial systems designed to operate in contested environments.

The company has positioned itself at the forefront of next-generation military drone development, emphasizing low-cost, high-performance autonomous systems that can be produced at scale. Kratos also provides satellite ground systems, space electronics, and microwave products that support both government and commercial space missions. Its focus on affordability and rapid production aligns with Pentagon priorities for mass and expendability in future conflicts.
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force Skyborg autonomous wingman program
U.S. Navy aerial target drone production
Satellite ground system contracts for DoD and commercial customers

name: Leonardo DRS
description:
Leonardo DRS is a U.S.-based defense electronics company that provides advanced sensing, network computing, force protection, and electric power technologies to military customers. A subsidiary of Italian defense giant Leonardo S.p.A., the company delivers naval electronics, ground combat systems, and networked computing solutions that support warfighters across all domains. Leonardo DRS is a key supplier of infrared sensors, radar systems, and naval power systems.

With heritage dating back to early defense electronics pioneers, Leonardo DRS has become a leading provider of tactical systems integration, power conversion, and advanced sensors for U.S. and allied forces. The company's products are embedded in major platforms including ships, ground vehicles, and aircraft, providing the sensing, computing, and power management capabilities essential to modern military operations.
keyProducts: Naval power systems and ship electronics
notableContracts: U.S. Navy ship electronics and power systems
U.S. Army combat vehicle modernization
Infrared sensor production for multiple platforms

name: BAE Systems Inc.
description:
BAE Systems Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of BAE Systems plc, one of the world's largest defense contractors. The U.S. operation provides electronic warfare systems, intelligence solutions, armored combat vehicles, naval ship repair, and advanced technology products to the Department of Defense and other government agencies. BAE Systems Inc. operates as a separate, ITAR-controlled entity to serve the U.S. national security market.

The company is a leader in electronic warfare, providing advanced EW systems for aircraft, ships, and ground forces. BAE Systems Inc. also manufactures armored vehicles including the Bradley Fighting Vehicle and Armored Multi-Purpose Vehicle (AMPV), operates major shipyards for naval repair and modernization, and delivers intelligence and surveillance solutions to defense and intelligence customers.
notableContracts: U.S. Army Armored Multi-Purpose Vehicle production
F-35 electronic warfare systems
U.S. Navy ship repair and maintenance contracts

name: General Atomics
description:
General Atomics is a defense and diversified technologies company best known for developing the Predator and Reaper unmanned aircraft systems that have transformed military ISR and strike operations. The company also conducts research in nuclear energy, electromagnetic systems, and advanced materials. Through its Aeronautical Systems subsidiary, General Atomics has become synonymous with military drones and remotely piloted aircraft.

Founded in 1955 as a nuclear research company, General Atomics has diversified into a major defense contractor while maintaining significant research activities in energy, physics, and advanced technologies. The company's MQ-9 Reaper and MQ-1C Gray Eagle are workhorses of U.S. military drone operations, while its electromagnetic systems division develops advanced launchers, railguns, and aircraft launch systems for naval applications.
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force MQ-9 Reaper production and sustainment
U.S. Army MQ-1C Gray Eagle Extended Range program
U.S. Navy EMALS and AAG for Ford-class carriers

name: Oshkosh Defense
description:
Oshkosh Defense is a leading manufacturer of tactical wheeled vehicles for the U.S. military and allied forces. A division of Oshkosh Corporation, the company produces the Joint Light Tactical Vehicle (JLTV), Family of Heavy Tactical Vehicles (FHTV), and a range of specialized military trucks. Oshkosh vehicles form the backbone of U.S. Army and Marine Corps ground transportation and logistics fleets.

With roots dating back to 1917 in the truck manufacturing industry, Oshkosh has become the premier supplier of tactical wheeled vehicles to the Department of Defense. The company's vehicles are known for their durability, mobility, and protection, designed to operate in the most demanding environments. Oshkosh also provides vehicle sustainment, modernization, and international sales support.
notableContracts: U.S. Army and Marine Corps JLTV production
U.S. Postal Service Next Generation Delivery Vehicle
International tactical vehicle sales and support

name: Curtiss-Wright Corporation
description:
Curtiss-Wright Corporation is a diversified industrial company that provides highly engineered products, solutions, and services to the aerospace, defense, and industrial markets. The company specializes in flight control systems, weapons handling equipment, sensors, naval propulsion components, and rugged electronics for military platforms. Curtiss-Wright products are found on aircraft, ships, submarines, and ground vehicles across all military services.

With heritage tracing back to aviation pioneers Glenn Curtiss and the Wright Brothers, Curtiss-Wright has evolved through decades of innovation and acquisition into a key supplier of mission-critical components and systems. The company's defense products include actuation systems for aircraft, weapons elevators for naval vessels, embedded computing solutions, and valve technologies for nuclear and conventional naval propulsion.
keyProducts: Aircraft actuation and flight control systems
Naval weapons handling and elevator systems
notableContracts: F-35 actuation systems and components
U.S. Navy carrier and submarine equipment
Commercial and military aircraft flight controls

name: Elbit Systems of America
description:
Elbit Systems of America is the U.S. subsidiary of Israeli defense company Elbit Systems Ltd., providing advanced defense electronics, systems integration, and homeland security solutions to U.S. government customers. The company specializes in night vision and electro-optical systems, C4I solutions, unmanned aircraft systems, and aircraft modernization. Elbit Systems of America serves the Department of Defense, Department of Homeland Security, and other federal agencies.

As a wholly owned U.S. subsidiary operating under Special Security Agreements, Elbit Systems of America brings Israeli defense innovation to the American market while meeting stringent ITAR requirements. The company is a major supplier of helmet-mounted displays, night vision goggles, and airborne systems, leveraging technology developed through decades of Israeli defense experience.
notableContracts: U.S. Army night vision goggle programs
F-35 helmet-mounted display system
Department of Homeland Security border surveillance

name: AAR Corp
description:
AAR Corp is an aviation services company that provides aftermarket support, MRO (maintenance, repair, and overhaul), and logistics services to commercial and government customers. The company's government business supports military aviation through parts supply, aircraft maintenance, fleet management, and expeditionary services. AAR operates globally with MRO facilities, distribution centers, and deployed support teams.

Founded in 1951, AAR has grown into a leading independent provider of aviation services with deep expertise in supply chain management and aircraft sustainment. The company supports U.S. military and allied air forces with parts distribution, heavy maintenance, modifications, and field services that keep aircraft mission-ready. AAR's commercial aviation business provides complementary scale and capabilities.
notableContracts: U.S. Air Force KC-135 tanker parts support
U.S. Army rotorcraft sustainment programs
Defense Logistics Agency aviation supply contracts

name: TransDigm Group
description:
TransDigm Group is a leading global designer, producer, and supplier of highly engineered aircraft components for commercial and military aircraft. The company owns a portfolio of proprietary aerospace businesses that manufacture critical components including actuators, batteries, ignition systems, pumps, valves, and cockpit displays. TransDigm products are installed on virtually every commercial and military aircraft platform.

TransDigm has grown primarily through acquisition, building a collection of aerospace component businesses with strong market positions and sole-source relationships. The company supplies components to both commercial airlines and defense customers, with military products found on fighters, bombers, tankers, helicopters, and transport aircraft across the U.S. military fleet.
notableContracts: Sole-source components for major military aircraft programs
F-35 Joint Strike Fighter component supply
Commercial and military aftermarket support

name: Austal USA
description:
Austal USA is an American shipbuilder and defense contractor that designs and constructs aluminum ships for the U.S. Navy and Coast Guard. A subsidiary of Australian shipbuilder Austal Limited, the company operates a major shipyard in Mobile, Alabama, where it builds Independence-class Littoral Combat Ships, Expeditionary Fast Transports (EPF), and other naval vessels. Austal USA has become a key player in the U.S. shipbuilding industrial base.

Founded in 1999 to bring Austal's high-speed aluminum vessel expertise to the U.S. market, the company has grown into a significant naval shipbuilder with capabilities spanning design, construction, and lifecycle support. Austal USA is expanding into steel shipbuilding and is competing for future Navy frigate and auxiliary ship programs, diversifying beyond its aluminum vessel heritage.
notableContracts: U.S. Navy Littoral Combat Ship production
U.S. Navy Expeditionary Fast Transport construction
U.S. Navy T-ATS Navajo-class construction

name: Bollinger Shipyards
description:
Bollinger Shipyards is a family-owned American shipbuilder that constructs patrol boats, cutters, and response vessels for the U.S. Coast Guard, Navy, and international customers. Based in Louisiana with multiple Gulf Coast facilities, Bollinger has delivered hundreds of vessels to government and commercial customers over its century-plus history. The company is a leading builder of small-to-medium surface combatants and patrol craft.

Founded in 1946, Bollinger has built its reputation on quality construction, on-time delivery, and strong relationships with government customers. The company has delivered numerous Fast Response Cutters to the Coast Guard and is expanding into larger vessels and Navy programs. Bollinger's workforce carries deep shipbuilding expertise passed down through generations of Louisiana craftsmen.
notableContracts: U.S. Coast Guard Fast Response Cutter program
U.S. Coast Guard medium and heavy cutters
U.S. Navy patrol and support craft

name: Fincantieri Marinette Marine
description:
Fincantieri Marinette Marine is an American shipbuilder and subsidiary of Italian shipbuilding giant Fincantieri. The company operates the Marinette Marine shipyard in Wisconsin, where it builds the U.S. Navy's Constellation-class guided-missile frigates, Freedom-class Littoral Combat Ships, and other naval vessels. Marinette Marine brings European frigate expertise to American shipbuilding.

With roots dating back to 1942, the Marinette shipyard has a long history of naval construction and is now at the center of the Navy's frigate recapitalization effort. The Constellation-class frigate, based on Fincantieri's proven FREMM design, represents a significant expansion of the company's role in the U.S. defense industrial base. The yard is undergoing major investments to support frigate production at scale.
notableContracts: U.S. Navy Constellation-class frigate program
U.S. Navy Freedom-class LCS construction
U.S. Coast Guard response boat production

name: Rocket Lab USA
description:
Rocket Lab USA is a space launch and satellite company that provides dedicated small launch services, spacecraft components, and space systems. The company operates the Electron rocket, the second most frequently launched U.S. rocket, and is developing the larger Neutron rocket for medium-lift missions. Rocket Lab also builds spacecraft and provides satellite components including solar panels, reaction wheels, and separation systems.

Founded in New Zealand and now headquartered in the United States, Rocket Lab has emerged as a leader in the small satellite launch market. The company serves defense, intelligence, and civil government customers with responsive launch capabilities and spacecraft solutions. Rocket Lab's vertically integrated approach enables rapid, affordable access to space for national security and commercial missions.
notableContracts: National Reconnaissance Office launch services
U.S. Space Force responsive launch programs
NASA CAPSTONE lunar mission

name: United Launch Alliance
description:
United Launch Alliance (ULA) is a joint venture between Boeing and Lockheed Martin that provides launch services for U.S. national security, civil, and commercial missions. ULA operates the Atlas V and Delta IV Heavy rockets and is developing the next-generation Vulcan Centaur launch system. The company has a perfect mission success record and is a trusted provider for the nation's most critical space missions.

Formed in 2006 to consolidate the legacy Atlas and Delta rocket programs, ULA has launched critical national security satellites, NASA science missions, and commercial payloads. The company is transitioning to the Vulcan Centaur rocket, which will provide enhanced capability and reduced cost for future missions. ULA's reliability and heritage make it a cornerstone of U.S. assured access to space.
notableContracts: National Security Space Launch Phase 2 contracts
NASA Commercial Crew and cargo missions
National Reconnaissance Office satellite launches

name: Aerojet Rocketdyne
description:
Aerojet Rocketdyne is a leading manufacturer of rocket propulsion systems, providing engines and propulsion solutions for space launch, strategic missiles, missile defense, and tactical systems. Now a subsidiary of L3Harris Technologies following acquisition, the company produces the RS-25 engines for NASA's Space Launch System, RL10 upper stage engines, and propulsion for numerous defense and space programs.

With heritage dating to the dawn of the space age, Aerojet Rocketdyne has powered some of America's most significant space achievements, from Apollo to the Space Shuttle to current exploration missions. The company also provides solid rocket motors for strategic deterrence systems and propulsion for missile defense interceptors, making it a critical supplier across the national security space and missile enterprise.
notableContracts: NASA Space Launch System RS-25 production
Missile Defense Agency interceptor propulsion
Strategic deterrence system propulsion

name: Maxar Technologies
description:
Maxar Technologies is a space technology company that provides Earth observation satellites, geospatial intelligence, and space infrastructure solutions to government and commercial customers. The company operates one of the world's most capable commercial imaging satellite constellations and provides analysis and services to defense and intelligence agencies. Maxar also manufactures spacecraft and space robotics systems.

Formed through the combination of DigitalGlobe, MDA, and SSL, Maxar brings together decades of expertise in satellite imaging, spacecraft manufacturing, and geospatial analytics. The company is a primary provider of commercial satellite imagery to the U.S. government and operates ground systems that support national security missions. Maxar's space infrastructure business builds communications and Earth observation satellites for government and commercial customers.
notableContracts: National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency EnhancedView/EOCL contracts
U.S. Space Force satellite manufacturing
NASA Lunar Gateway power and propulsion element

name: Ball Aerospace
description:
Ball Aerospace is a subsidiary of Ball Corporation that designs and manufactures spacecraft, instruments, sensors, and systems for civil, commercial, and national security space applications. The company is known for building critical optical systems and spacecraft, including mirrors for the James Webb Space Telescope and instruments for numerous NASA science missions. Ball also provides tactical solutions and antenna systems for defense applications.

With over six decades of aerospace heritage, Ball Aerospace has contributed to some of the most important space science and national security missions in history. The company excels in developing advanced optics, cryogenic systems, and precision instruments that push the boundaries of space-based observation. Ball also provides ground systems, data exploitation, and operational support for government space programs.
notableContracts: NASA James Webb Space Telescope mirrors
NASA science mission instruments
National security space systems and sensors

name: AM General
description:
AM General is an American defense contractor best known as the manufacturer of the High Mobility Multipurpose Wheeled Vehicle (HMMWV), commonly known as the Humvee. The company has produced over 300,000 Humvees for the U.S. military and allied nations, making it one of the most prolific military vehicles in history. AM General continues to manufacture, modernize, and support the Humvee fleet while pursuing next-generation vehicle programs.

Originally part of American Motors Corporation, AM General became an independent military vehicle manufacturer with a singular focus on designing and building vehicles for defense customers. The company's South Bend, Indiana facility remains the center of Humvee production and supports a global installed base of vehicles with parts, upgrades, and technical services.
notableContracts: U.S. Army and Marine Corps HMMWV production
Humvee modernization and recapitalization programs
Foreign Military Sales vehicle contracts

name: Fluor Corporation
description:
Fluor Corporation is one of the world's largest engineering, procurement, construction, and maintenance companies, with a significant government segment that supports U.S. military and federal agency missions. Fluor provides base operations, logistics, construction, and contingency support services through contracts like LOGCAP and supports Department of Energy nuclear facilities and environmental remediation projects.

Founded in 1912, Fluor has grown into a global infrastructure and services company with deep capabilities in complex project execution. The company's government business supports military readiness through base operations, facilities management, and deployed logistics support, while also managing critical nuclear and environmental programs for the Department of Energy.
notableContracts: U.S. Army LOGCAP task orders
Department of Energy site management contracts
Nuclear and environmental remediation programs

name: AECOM
description:
AECOM is a global infrastructure consulting firm that provides planning, design, engineering, and program management services to government and commercial clients. The company's federal and defense practice supports military construction, base operations, environmental services, and infrastructure projects for the Department of Defense, Department of Energy, and other agencies.

Headquartered in Texas, AECOM has grown through acquisitions into one of the world's largest engineering and construction management firms. The company supports defense customers with facility design, environmental compliance, program management, and technical services that enable military readiness and modernization.
notableContracts: Department of Defense facility design and construction
Department of Energy environmental services
Military base operations and maintenance

name: Day & Zimmermann
description:
Day & Zimmermann is a family-owned company that provides munitions manufacturing, maintenance, and staffing services to the U.S. Department of Defense and commercial customers. The company operates Army Ammunition Plants, provides depot-level maintenance for military equipment, and offers technical staffing services. Day & Zimmermann is one of the largest private munitions manufacturers in the United States.

Founded in 1901, Day & Zimmermann has evolved through generations of family ownership into a diversified services company with deep expertise in defense manufacturing and maintenance. The company operates government-owned facilities that produce ammunition and explosives, maintains military vehicles and equipment, and provides skilled technical workers to defense and industrial customers.
notableContracts: Army Ammunition Plant operations
U.S. Army depot maintenance contracts
Munitions demilitarization programs

name: Teledyne Technologies
description:
Teledyne Technologies is a diversified industrial company that provides sophisticated instrumentation, digital imaging, aerospace and defense electronics, and engineered systems to government and commercial customers. The company's defense portfolio includes marine systems, electronic warfare components, imaging sensors, and test and measurement equipment. Teledyne significantly expanded its defense capabilities through the acquisition of FLIR Systems.

Founded in 1960, Teledyne has evolved through organic growth and strategic acquisitions into a leading provider of enabling technologies for defense, space, and industrial markets. The company's products include underwater vehicles, thermal imaging systems, aerospace electronics, and precision sensors that support military and intelligence operations across all domains.
notableContracts: U.S. military thermal imaging and ISR systems
Navy underwater vehicle programs
Space and missile defense electronics

name: Akima
description:
Akima is an Alaska Native Corporation-owned federal contractor that provides IT, facilities management, logistics, and professional services to defense and civilian government customers. As a subsidiary of NANA Regional Corporation, Akima leverages Alaska Native Corporation contracting preferences while delivering mission-critical services across the federal enterprise.

Akima has grown into one of the largest Alaska Native Corporation contractors, operating through multiple subsidiary companies that specialize in IT services, base operations, construction, and technical support. The company serves all branches of the military, intelligence agencies, and civilian departments with services that enable mission success and operational readiness.
notableContracts: Department of Defense IT support contracts
Federal facility operations and maintenance
Military base support services

name: Chenega Corporation
description:
Chenega Corporation is an Alaska Native Corporation that provides professional, technical, and security services to federal government customers through its subsidiary companies. Chenega's federal services portfolio includes IT, cybersecurity, intelligence support, logistics, and protective services for defense and civilian agencies.

Based in Anchorage with operations throughout the United States and overseas, Chenega has grown from its Alaska Native village roots into a major federal contractor. The corporation uses its 8(a) and Alaska Native Corporation contracting advantages to compete for and win significant government contracts while reinvesting profits to benefit Alaska Native shareholders and communities.
notableContracts: Department of Defense IT and cyber contracts
Intelligence Community mission support
Federal protective services contracts

name: CGI Federal
description:
CGI Federal is the U.S. federal subsidiary of CGI Inc., a Canadian IT services company. CGI Federal provides IT consulting, systems integration, and managed services to defense, intelligence, and civilian government customers. The company specializes in enterprise IT modernization, cybersecurity, data analytics, and digital transformation for federal agencies.

As a wholly owned subsidiary operating under a Proxy Agreement, CGI Federal serves the U.S. government while leveraging the global capabilities and scale of its parent company. CGI Federal supports defense customers with mission IT, network modernization, and enterprise solutions that enable digital transformation and operational efficiency.
notableContracts: Department of Defense enterprise IT programs
Federal civilian agency modernization
Veterans Affairs IT support

name: Guidehouse
description:
Guidehouse is a management consulting firm that provides advisory, managed services, and technology solutions to government and commercial clients. The company's defense and national security practice supports the Department of Defense with strategy, financial management, supply chain, and technology consulting. Guidehouse combines traditional management consulting with technology implementation capabilities.

Formed from the public sector consulting business of PricewaterhouseCoopers and expanded through acquisitions, Guidehouse has become a leading provider of consulting services to federal agencies. The company helps defense customers improve operations, manage transformation, and implement technology solutions that enhance mission effectiveness and efficiency.
notableContracts: Department of Defense financial management
Federal agency transformation programs
Defense supply chain consulting

name: Telos Corporation
description:
Telos Corporation provides cybersecurity, secure communications, and identity management solutions to federal government and commercial customers. The company specializes in security assessments, vulnerability management, and secure network solutions that protect critical government systems and infrastructure.

Founded in 1968, Telos has evolved from a systems integrator into a focused cybersecurity and secure communications company. The company provides automated security compliance tools, identity verification services, and secure mobile communications that help government and commercial customers protect sensitive information and systems from cyber threats.
notableContracts: Department of Defense cybersecurity programs
Federal identity management services
Intelligence Community security support

name: Cubic Corporation
description:
Cubic Corporation provides mission and training solutions, C4ISR systems, and transportation services to defense and commercial customers. The company's defense business specializes in live, virtual, and constructive training systems, air and ground combat training ranges, and tactical communications and networking solutions for military customers worldwide.

With roots in defense electronics dating to the 1950s, Cubic has developed deep expertise in training and readiness solutions that prepare military forces for combat. The company's training ranges and systems are used by U.S. and allied forces globally, while its C4ISR products provide situational awareness and communications capabilities for tactical operations.
notableContracts: National Training Center and JRTC instrumentation
Air combat training systems worldwide
Tactical data link and C4ISR programs

name: Scientific Research Corporation
description:
Scientific Research Corporation (SRC) is an employee-owned company that provides advanced defense electronics, radar systems, electronic warfare solutions, and C4ISR capabilities to the U.S. military and intelligence community. The company develops innovative sensing, communications, and information systems that protect warfighters and enable mission success.

Founded in 1980, SRC has grown into a leading provider of cutting-edge defense electronics and systems integration services. The company is known for rapid development of prototype systems and transition to fielded capabilities, often working on the most challenging technical problems in radar, EW, and signal processing for defense and intelligence customers.
notableContracts: U.S. Army counter-UAS and radar programs
Department of Defense electronic warfare contracts
Intelligence Community C4ISR support

name: Battelle Memorial Institute
description:
Battelle Memorial Institute is an independent nonprofit applied science and technology development organization that manages national laboratories and conducts research for government and commercial clients. The organization is the largest nonprofit research and development contractor in the world, managing or co-managing major national laboratories including Oak Ridge, Pacific Northwest, Brookhaven, and Idaho National Laboratory.

Founded in 1929, Battelle has played a central role in American science and technology development, contributing to innovations from xerography to optical digital recording. The organization's defense work spans chemical and biological defense, cybersecurity, advanced materials, and systems engineering, supporting the Department of Defense, intelligence community, and Department of Energy with applied research and technology development.
notableContracts: Department of Energy national laboratory management
Department of Defense R&D contracts
CBRN defense research programs

name: Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory
description:
Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) is a university-affiliated research center that solves complex national security, space exploration, and scientific challenges. APL is a trusted technical adviser to government sponsors, providing independent engineering analysis, systems development, and operational support for critical defense and space programs.

Founded in 1942 during World War II to develop the proximity fuze, APL has continued to deliver breakthrough innovations for national security. The laboratory is a leader in missile defense technology, space exploration systems, undersea warfare, and cyber operations, providing the government with technical expertise and innovative solutions that often cannot be obtained elsewhere.
notableContracts: Missile Defense Agency technical direction and engineering
NASA planetary science missions
U.S. Navy undersea warfare programs

name: Firefly Aerospace
description:
Firefly Aerospace is a space launch and spacecraft company that provides launch services, lunar landers, and space systems for government and commercial customers. The company operates the Alpha small launch vehicle and is developing the medium-lift MLV rocket. Firefly is also building lunar landers under NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services program.

Based in Texas, Firefly has emerged as a promising entrant in the space launch market, offering responsive and affordable access to orbit. The company serves defense, civil, and commercial customers with launch services and is expanding into spacecraft development and lunar surface delivery.
notableContracts: NASA Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS)
U.S. Space Force launch services
Commercial satellite launches

name: Relativity Space
description:
Relativity Space is a space launch company that uses 3D printing technology to manufacture rockets, dramatically reducing the complexity and cost of launch vehicle production. The company is developing the Terran R medium-lift rocket, a fully reusable launch vehicle designed to compete for commercial and government launch contracts.

Founded in 2015, Relativity has pioneered the use of large-scale additive manufacturing for aerospace applications, developing the world's largest metal 3D printers. The company's approach promises faster iteration, lower costs, and ultimately the ability to manufacture rockets on Mars. Relativity serves both commercial and government customers seeking innovative and cost-effective launch solutions.
notableContracts: Commercial satellite launch contracts
OneWeb satellite launches
Government and defense launch opportunities

name: Vista Outdoor
description:
Vista Outdoor is an outdoor sports and recreation company whose ammunition division, Federal Premium, is a major supplier of small-caliber ammunition to the U.S. military and law enforcement. The company manufactures rifle, pistol, and shotgun ammunition for both military and commercial markets through its Federal, CCI, Speer, and Remington ammunition brands.

Spun off from Alliant Techsystems (ATK) in 2015, Vista Outdoor has focused on the consumer outdoor products market while maintaining significant military ammunition production capabilities. Federal Premium has supplied ammunition to the U.S. military for decades and remains a key domestic source for small arms ammunition used in training and combat operations.
notableContracts: U.S. Army small-caliber ammunition supply
Department of Defense training ammunition
Law enforcement ammunition contracts

name: Vigor Industrial
description:
Vigor Industrial is a shipbuilder and industrial fabricator based in the Pacific Northwest that provides ship construction, repair, and maintenance services to the U.S. Navy, Coast Guard, and commercial customers. The company operates shipyards in Oregon, Washington, and Alaska, supporting both new construction and lifecycle maintenance for naval and commercial vessels.

With roots dating back to the early 20th century, Vigor has become the leading shipyard in the Pacific Northwest, providing critical infrastructure for naval ship maintenance on the West Coast. The company also provides industrial fabrication services for aerospace and infrastructure projects, leveraging its heavy manufacturing capabilities across multiple facilities.
notableContracts: U.S. Navy ship repair and maintenance
U.S. Coast Guard vessel support
NOAA ship maintenance

name: Navistar Defense
description:
Navistar Defense is a leading provider of tactical wheeled vehicles and sustainment services to the U.S. military and allied forces. The company manufactures the MaxxPro MRAP (Mine Resistant Ambush Protected) vehicle and a range of medium tactical trucks based on its International truck platforms. Now a subsidiary of TRATON GROUP following acquisition, Navistar Defense continues to support a large installed base of vehicles worldwide.

Building on International Truck's heritage of rugged commercial vehicles, Navistar Defense has become a significant supplier of protected vehicles that save lives on the battlefield. The company's MRAP vehicles were deployed extensively in Iraq and Afghanistan, and it continues to provide new production, modernization, and sustainment services for tactical vehicle fleets.
notableContracts: U.S. Army and Marine Corps MRAP programs
International MRAP sales and support
Medium tactical vehicle supply contracts

name: Carahsoft Technology Corp
description:
Carahsoft Technology Corp is the largest government IT distributor and solutions provider in the United States, serving as a master aggregator and channel partner for hundreds of technology vendors selling to federal, state, and local government customers. The company provides procurement vehicle access, contract management, and go-to-market services that enable technology companies to sell to the government.

Founded in 2004, Carahsoft has become an essential intermediary in the government technology market, helping both established and emerging technology vendors navigate complex government procurement processes. The company holds contracts on virtually every major government-wide acquisition contract vehicle and provides the infrastructure that enables efficient technology acquisition across the public sector.
notableContracts: GSA Schedule and GWAC contract vehicles
NASA SEWP contract
State and local government contracts

name: Olin Corporation
description:
Olin Corporation is a diversified industrial company whose Winchester division is one of the world's largest manufacturers of small-caliber ammunition for military, law enforcement, and commercial markets. Winchester has supplied ammunition to the U.S. military for over a century and operates the Lake City Army Ammunition Plant under contract to the U.S. Army.

While Olin's primary business is chemicals, its Winchester ammunition division maintains critical defense industrial base capabilities. The Lake City plant is the primary source of small-caliber ammunition for the U.S. military, producing billions of rounds annually. Winchester's military ammunition supports training and combat operations for all branches of the armed forces.
keyProducts: Lake City Army Ammunition Plant operations
notableContracts: Lake City Army Ammunition Plant operations
U.S. Army small-caliber ammunition production
Department of Defense training ammunition supply

name: Caliburn International
description:
Caliburn International is a professional services company that provides medical, security, and contingency services to government customers. The company supports the Department of Defense, Department of Homeland Security, and Department of State with healthcare services, security operations, and specialized mission support in challenging environments.

Formed through the combination of Comprehensive Health Services, Janus Global Operations, and other service providers, Caliburn delivers critical support services in deployed environments, at government facilities, and in contingency operations worldwide. The company is known for its medical services capabilities, providing healthcare to military personnel, government employees, and others in austere locations.
notableContracts: Department of Defense medical support contracts
Department of Homeland Security healthcare services
Department of State security and medical programs

name: Modern Technology Solutions Inc.
description:
Modern Technology Solutions, Inc. (MTSI) is an employee-owned engineering and technology services company that provides systems engineering, test and evaluation, and technical advisory services to defense and intelligence customers. The company specializes in hypersonics, directed energy, space systems, and advanced weapons development, supporting the most challenging technical programs in the Department of Defense.

Founded in 1993, MTSI has built a reputation for technical excellence and independent analysis that helps government customers develop and field advanced capabilities. The company's engineers and scientists work on cutting-edge programs in hypersonics, missile defense, and emerging technologies, providing the technical depth that supports successful program execution.
notableContracts: Department of Defense hypersonics programs
Missile Defense Agency technical support
U.S. Air Force advanced weapons development

name: MITRE Corporation
description:
MITRE Corporation is a nonprofit organization that operates federally funded research and development centers (FFRDCs) and provides systems engineering, research, and advisory services to government sponsors. MITRE manages FFRDCs for the Department of Defense, Federal Aviation Administration, IRS, Department of Homeland Security, and other agencies, serving as an independent trusted adviser on complex technical and policy challenges.

Founded in 1958, MITRE traces its origins to MIT Lincoln Laboratory and has played a central role in developing critical national capabilities including air defense systems, FAA air traffic control, and cybersecurity frameworks. As a not-for-profit, MITRE operates in the public interest, providing unbiased technical analysis and systems engineering expertise that government agencies cannot obtain from commercial contractors.
notableContracts: Department of Defense FFRDC operations
FAA Center for Advanced Aviation System Development
DHS Homeland Security Systems Engineering and Development Institute

name: Rebellion Defense
description:
Rebellion Defense is a technology company that builds AI and machine learning tools to help defense and national security organizations make faster, better-informed decisions. The company focuses on operationalizing AI for military applications, including predictive maintenance, threat assessment, and decision support systems.

Founded by former government officials with deep national security experience, Rebellion Defense brings a mission-focused approach to defense AI development. The company works closely with military customers to understand operational challenges and deploy practical AI solutions that enhance readiness and effectiveness.
notableContracts: Department of Defense AI pilot programs
Military predictive maintenance contracts
National security analytics support

name: Saab, Inc.
description:
Saab, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of Swedish defense company Saab AB, providing advanced defense systems and solutions to the U.S. military and federal agencies. The company manufactures the Carl-Gustaf recoilless rifle system, the AT4 anti-armor weapon, and other ground combat systems at its U.S. facilities. Saab also provides command and control, training, and simulation solutions.

Operating as a U.S. company under Special Security Agreements, Saab has established significant manufacturing presence in the United States, producing weapons and systems for both U.S. forces and export customers. The company brings Swedish defense innovation to the American market while meeting domestic production and security requirements.
notableContracts: U.S. Army Carl-Gustaf and AT4 production
Foreign Military Sales weapons programs
Training system development contracts
